name: Lift-to-Drag Ratio Calculator
description: Compute aerodynamic efficiency using lift and drag forces.
formulaDescription: The lift-to-drag ratio is a measure of how efficiently an aircraft generates lift relative to drag.

example.explanation: A lift of 4500 N against 130 N drag gives a lift-to-drag ratio of about 34.6.

faqs.question: Why is lift-to-drag ratio important?
faqs.answer: Higher lift-to-drag ratios mean more efficient flight and better glide performance.
